description: FULL OUTER JOIN makes columns from BOTH sides nullable

columns.name: user_id
columns.note: FULL JOIN: left side becomes nullable

columns.name: name
columns.note: FULL JOIN: left side becomes nullable

columns.name: order_id
columns.note: FULL JOIN: right side becomes nullable

columns.name: total
columns.note: FULL JOIN: right side becomes nullable

name: basic_full_join
query_sql:
-- @name GetAllUsersAndOrders
-- @returns :many
SELECT u.id AS user_id, u.name, o.id AS order_id, o.total FROM users u FULL OUTER JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', bio TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), total INTEGER NOT NULL, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe correctly marks ALL columns from both sides as nullable due to FULL OUTER JOIN semantics
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks all columns as non-nullable based on schema definitions
